commit.message:
feat: add SPLIT_ROUTING toggle — route all traffic through proxy (#29)

Adds a `SPLIT_ROUTING` env var (default `true`) to disable geo-based
split routing entirely, sending all traffic through the proxy outbound.

## Changes

- **`config_generator.py`** — `ConfigSettings` gets `split_route: bool =
True`; when `False`, `_build_dns` emits no direct-site DNS rules and
`_build_route` drops all geo/custom direct rules and `rule_set` entries
(only `sniff`, `hijack-dns`, and private-IP-direct remain)
- **`sync.py`** — reads `SPLIT_ROUTING` env var, passes `split_route` to
`ConfigSettings`; skips `ensure_rule_sets` entirely when split routing
is off
- **`tui.py`** — `SPLIT_ROUTING` added to `EDITABLE_KEYS` in
`ConfigScreen`; `NodesScreen` node-switch respects the setting
- **`config.env.example`** — documents `SPLIT_ROUTING=true` (commented
out)

Set `SPLIT_ROUTING=false` (also accepts `0` / `no`) to proxy all
traffic.

commit.message:
feat: custom geo rule set URLs for direct routing (RULE_SET_DIRECT_IP_URLS / RULE_SET_DIRECT_SITE_URLS) (#28)

Extends geo rule set support beyond SagerNet's sing-geoip/sing-geosite
to allow arbitrary `.srs` sources (e.g. AntifilterCommunity for RKN
bypass, XTLS/geoip, Loyalsoldier/sing-box-rules).

## What and why

Two new env vars accept `tag:https://url,...` pairs. On sync, files are
downloaded to `RULE_SET_DIR/{tag}.srs` if absent, then wired into
sing-box config as `local` rule sets:

- `RULE_SET_DIRECT_IP_URLS` — IP-based: routing only
- `RULE_SET_DIRECT_SITE_URLS` — domain-based: DNS (route → local) +
routing

```bash
# config.env — bypass RKN-blocked domains/IPs without proxying
RULE_SET_DIRECT_SITE_URLS=antifilter-community:https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AntifilterCommunity/antifilter-ruleset/main/antifilter-community.srs
RULE_SET_DIRECT_IP_URLS=antifilter-ip-community:https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AntifilterCommunity/antifilter-ruleset/main/antifilter-ip-community.srs
```

**Changes:**
- `config_generator.py` — `ConfigSettings` gains
`custom_direct_ip_rule_sets` / `custom_direct_site_rule_sets`
(`List[Tuple[str, str]]`); `_build_route` and `_build_dns` emit entries
for them
- `binary_manager.py` — `ensure_rule_sets` accepts and downloads custom
rule set URL pairs
- `sync.py` — `_parse_rule_set_urls` parses the env vars;
`ConfigSettings` and `ensure_rule_sets` calls updated
- `config.env.example` / `README.md` — documented with
AntifilterCommunity example URLs

Fully backward-compatible; existing `GEO_DIRECT_IP`/`GEO_DIRECT_SITE`
behaviour unchanged.

## Type
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Docs / cleanup

## Checklist

- [ ] Tested on actual VyOS (or clearly noted as untested)
- [x] No hardcoded paths / credentials

commit.message:
fix: sing-box binary auto-download on container start/update and install (#27)

`binary_manager.ensure_sing_box/ensure_rule_sets` were defined but never
called — `sync.py` only validated binary presence and hard-failed. On a
fresh container or after an image update (binary not in any volume),
this caused an unhandled `FileNotFoundError` that silently killed the
sing-box thread.

## What and why

**Root causes:**
- `sync.py` checked for binary existence but never downloaded it;
`binary_manager.py` was dead code
- Binary/ruleset download happened *after* the `SUBSCRIPTION_URL` guard,
so a fresh install with unconfigured URL never got the binary either
- `Popen` raising `OSError` in `_run_sing_box` bypassed the restart
counter entirely, leaving the daemon running with no sing-box and no
stop signal
- `install.sh` would abort via `set -e` if first sync failed (e.g.
`SUBSCRIPTION_URL` not yet set), skipping service enablement
- `sing-box.service` hardcoded `/usr/local/bin/sing-box` instead of
reading `XRAY_BIN` from the env file

**Changes:**

- **`src/sync.py`** — wire in `ensure_sing_box()` +
`ensure_rule_sets()`; move both calls *before* the `SUBSCRIPTION_URL`
check so the binary is always fetched regardless of subscription state
- **`src/daemon.py`** — wrap `subprocess.Popen` in `try/except OSError`;
launch failures now log, set `ret = 127`, and feed into the existing
restart/stop logic
- **`install.sh`** — make initial sync soft-fail (`|| echo ...`); binary
is downloaded as a side-effect even when URL isn't configured yet, and
services are always enabled
- **`systemd/sing-box.service`** — add
`EnvironmentFile=-/etc/remnaproxy/config.env` with fallback
`Environment=` defaults; use `$XRAY_BIN`/`$XRAY_CONFIG` in `Exec*` lines
- **`config.env.example`** — add comment that `XRAY_BIN` is
auto-downloaded on first sync if missing

## Type
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Docs / cleanup

## Checklist
- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -v` passes
- [ ] Tested on actual VyOS (or clearly noted as untested)
- [x] No hardcoded paths / credentials

fix: add sourceToolAssistantUUID to Claude CLI JSONL tool-result events

Claude CLI validates tool-use relationships locally before making any API
call. When a user event contains tool_result blocks, it expects a
sourceToolAssistantUUID field pointing to the assistant event that issued
the corresponding tool_use blocks. Without this field, Claude CLI reports
"API Error: 400 due to tool use concurrency issues" immediately at resume
time (duration_api_ms=0 — no API call is made).

Track the UUID of the last assistant event that contained tool_use blocks
and attach it as sourceToolAssistantUUID on the following user event when
it contains tool_result blocks. This matches native Claude CLI JSONL format.

commit.message:
fix: split interleaved tool_use/text into proper Anthropic API turns

A single v2 assistant message can contain sequential tool calls and text
from multiple turns (tool A → result → text → tool B → result → text).
The old exporter put ALL tool_use and text blocks into one assistant
message, creating interleaved patterns like [tool_use, text, tool_use,
text] that the Anthropic API rejects with a 400 error.

Now text blocks following tool_use blocks trigger a turn split:
- Each group of tool_uses becomes a separate assistant+user(results) pair
- Text after tool results starts a new assistant turn
- Parallel tool calls (consecutive tool_uses without text) stay together

Also refactored AnthropicClientExporter to reduce cognitive complexity
by extracting helper methods and string constants.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: filter empty text content blocks from Claude CLI session export

EntryDataConverter.fromMessages() intentionally inserts empty EntryData.Text
entries for UI rendering when assistant messages contain only tool calls.
These empty text blocks were faithfully serialized through toMessages() →
toAnthropicMessages() → JSONL export, producing {"type":"text","text":""}
blocks that the Anthropic API rejects with HTTP 400: "text content blocks
must be non-empty".

Filter out empty text blocks in toAnthropicMessages() for both user and
assistant messages. Also remove redundant 'continue' in the skip-types
else-if branch.

commit.message:
fix: preserve real message timestamps through session export pipeline

Claude CLI branches from wrong message when all events share the same
timestamp. Root cause: EntryDataConverter.toMessages() used
System.currentTimeMillis() for every message, and ClaudeCliExporter
used Instant.now() for all events — both discarding the original
EntryData timestamps.

Three fixes:
- EntryDataConverter.toMessages(): extract ISO timestamps from EntryData
  entries and parse them to epoch millis for SessionMessage.createdAt
- EntryDataConverter.fromMessages(): restore SessionMessage.createdAt
  to ISO strings when rebuilding EntryData (was always empty "")
- ClaudeCliExporter: use per-message createdAt via AnthropicMessage
  instead of a single Instant.now() for the entire export

Also added createdAt field to AnthropicMessage so timestamps flow
through toAnthropicMessages() and mergeConsecutiveSameRole() naturally.

commit.message:
fix: enable serializeNulls in ClaudeCliExporter Gson instance

Gson 2.13.1 silently drops JsonNull.INSTANCE entries from JsonObject
during serialization. This caused the root user message to omit
"parentUuid": null, which Claude CLI requires to identify the
conversation root when building the message tree for --resume.

Without "parentUuid": null on the root node, the CLI cannot find the
tree root and branches from the first user message instead of
continuing from the last, causing the conversation to fork.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: re-export session on same-agent reconnect for Claude CLI resume

When a user picks a session from the dropdown and reconnects to the same
agent (e.g. Claude → Claude), connectToAgent() skips switchAgent() since
the profile hasn't changed. This means doExport() never runs, and
createSession() reads a stale cliResumeSessionId that points to an old
JSONL file — possibly exported with a buggy last-prompt pointing to the
first message instead of the last.

Fix:
- For Latest/Older session choices on same-agent reconnect, call
  exportForRestart() to re-export the current v2 session with the
  correct last-prompt and parentUuid chain.
- For "None" (fresh session), clear the Claude CLI resume state so
  --resume is not passed.
- Add clearClaudeResumeState() to SessionSwitchService to clear both
  the PropertiesComponent value and the file-based fallback.

fix: use last user prompt text in Claude CLI last-prompt event

The `last-prompt` JSONL event was using the FIRST user message text
(`extractFirstUserPromptText`) instead of the LAST. This caused Claude
CLI's `--resume` to fork a new branch from the first user message in
the conversation tree, losing all subsequent context.

Native CLI behavior: after each prompt cycle, the CLI writes
`last-prompt` with the text of the prompt it just processed. On resume,
it finds the matching user message and continues from there.

Our export was pointing the CLI at the very first message, causing it
to branch from the root with a synthetic "No response requested."
assistant message, effectively discarding the entire conversation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Claude CLI session export format to match native JSONL structure

Three root-cause fixes for --resume forking from the wrong message:

1. parentUuid: null on root node — use JsonNull.INSTANCE so Gson actually
   serializes the key. Without it, the CLI can't build the message tree
   and falls back to a synthetic branch from the first event.

2. last-prompt uses FIRST user message text — native CLI records the
   initial prompt of the conversation cycle, not the last user message.
   Changed extractLastUserPromptText → extractFirstUserPromptText.

3. version field — detect real CLI version from latest native session
   file in the same project directory (fallback: "2.1.78"). The old
   hardcoded "1.0.0" is not a real CLI version.

Additional improvements:
- Add gitBranch field to all events (read from .git/HEAD)
- Add stop_sequence: null to assistant messages via JsonNull.INSTANCE
- Introduce EventContext record to reduce messageEvent parameter count
- Extract FIELD_VERSION constant for duplicate literal

commit.message:
fix: add last-prompt entry to Claude CLI exported sessions

Claude CLI uses a `last-prompt` event at the end of a session file to track
the conversation head when resuming via --resume. Without it, Claude creates
a synthetic assistant branch from the first user message, ignoring the exported
conversation history entirely (Bug 29).

The fix adds extractLastUserPromptText() to find the last text-bearing user
message, and appends {"type":"last-prompt","lastPrompt":"...","sessionId":"..."}
at the end of every exported session. With this entry, Claude resumes from
after the last assistant response, correctly inheriting the full context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Claude session resume lost on plugin hot-reload

PropertiesComponent values set during dispose() are lost on plugin
hot-reload because IntelliJ flushes project state to disk before
calling dispose(), so in-memory-only changes are discarded.

Add a file-based fallback (.agent-work/sessions/claude-resume-id.txt)
that persists the Claude CLI resume session ID to disk during export.
ClaudeCliClient.createSession() reads this file when PropertiesComponent
has no value, ensuring --resume is passed after hot-reload.

Also reset PromptOrchestrator session state on agent switch so
createSession() is called for the new client, consuming the resume ID.
